slides.title: Strength is built on your *rest days*.
slides.sub: Training breaks you down. Recovery is where the body rebuilds stronger. Both matter for aging well.

slides.title: Training is the stress
slides.body: A hard session is a signal, not the result. It asks the body to adapt, but the *real change* happens afterward.

slides.title: Recovery is the growth
slides.body: Muscle, heart and nervous system rebuild while you rest. Skip recovery and you train the stress *without the reward*.

slides.title: Signs you under-recover
slides.body: Flat workouts, poor sleep, low mood and lingering soreness can be quiet signs your body is *asking for rest*.

slides.title: Build in real rest
slides.body: Plan easy days like you plan hard ones. Gentle movement, good sleep and food can support a stronger comeback.

slides.title: Rest is part of training
slides.body: You don't get strong despite rest days. You get strong *because of them*. Calm consistency outlasts constant grinding.

slides.title: Let your body *rebuild stronger*.
slides.body: Vibe Health weighs your sleep, movement and recovery into one daily score, so you can see when to push and when to rest.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Strength isn't built in the workout. It's built in the rest that follows. 🌿

Training is the stress, recovery is the adaptation. Flat sessions, poor sleep and lingering soreness are signs to ease off. Plan rest like you plan effort. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns sleep, movement and recovery into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Recovery and Rest Days Build Real Strength
